label: Fault Injection -> Automatic Localization
summary: A compressed presentation packet built from real control-loop artifacts. It shows one injected deny storm moving from deterministic detection to operator-readable localization.

source.note: Absolute timestamps are compressed for demo clarity. The underlying alert, suggestion, and replay stats come from repository control-loop history.

currentSlice.title: Repeated denied udp/3702 traffic is localized to Dahua camera 8L10584PAJ33778
currentSlice.focus: The operator does not have to decode a raw rule string. The output already identifies the affected device class, the exact path, the recurrence level, and the next checks to run.

timeline.title: Fault injected on camera discovery path
timeline.detail: A short deny storm is intentionally created on udp/3702 from the Dahua camera to produce a visible incident without touching the inference path.

timeline.title: Deterministic rule crosses threshold
timeline.detail: core/correlator emits alert 8daf1f79... after observing 3256 denies in 60 seconds against a 200 deny threshold.

timeline.title: Automatic localization assembles the incident surface
timeline.detail: The evidence bundle ties the alert to device 8L10584PAJ33778, vendor Dahua, role IP Camera, source 192.168.1.24, destination 192.168.2.108, and zone lan.

timeline.title: Change and recurrence signals strengthen the case
timeline.detail: recent_similar_1h=284 and suspected_change=true make the alert more actionable than a bare threshold breach.

timeline.title: Suggested operator response is ready
timeline.detail: The UI can immediately show session-trace inspection, recent deny history, and service/path validation as the first response options.

recommendedActions: Inspect the source device session trace and recent deny history in ClickHouse.
Check whether the affected service is expected for this device profile and interface path.
If this repeats, compare it with the cluster-level suggestion stream before tuning thresholds.
presenterNotes: Lead with the fact that the anomaly proof is deterministic, not model-generated.
Then point out that the model stage adds localization and operator guidance, not existence proof.
If you need a second beat, mention that the replay validator saw 12751 cluster triggers and udp/3702 was the top repeated service.
